name: Suspicious Archive Creation in Staging Directory

queries.spl:
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4688
| where match(ProcessName, "(?i)(7z|winrar|rar\.exe|zip\.exe)")
| where match(CommandLine, "(?i)(\\temp\\|\\appdata\\")
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where FileName in~ ("7z.exe","winrar.exe","rar.exe")
| where ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(\\temp\\|\\appdata\\)"
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule tde_col_001 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Suspicious Archive Creation in Staging Directory"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0009"
    technique = "T1560.001"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4688
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE

name: Bulk File Access to Sensitive Directories

queries.spl:
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4663
| where match(ObjectName, "(?i)(\\finance\\|\\hr\\|\\payroll\\|\\confidential\\")
| bucket _time span=30m
| stats dc(ObjectName) as files by SubjectUserName, _time
| where files >= 100
| table _time, SubjectUserName, files
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID == 4663
| where ObjectName matches regex @"(?i)(\\finance\\|\\hr\\|\\payroll\\|\\confidential\\)"
| summarize Files = dcount(ObjectName) by SubjectUserName, bin(TimeGenerated, 30m)
| where Files >= 100
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount

WHERE eventid = 4663
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)

    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4663')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4663])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4663
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:

| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4663)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

name: SharePoint File Shared Outside Organization
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a file that is shared with a user that is outside the organization which could allow for unauthorized access by outside users to sensitive data.
pseudo_logic: 1 event where the operation is "SharingInvitationCreated" and the workload is not "OneDrive" and the target user is not a domain owned by the organization

queries.spl:
index=windows
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, ClientIP, Operation, OfficeWorkload
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_col_000026 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "SharePoint File Shared Outside Organization"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0009"
    technique = "T1537"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Mass File Download
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a user downloads multiple files from a file transfer/storage appliance. This could indicate a data exfiltration attempt by insider threat or a compromised user.
pseudo_logic:
20 Sharepoint Events
Where the Operation is "FileDownloaded"
Where the workload is "Sharepoint"
With distinct File Names
With the same Source Username 
Within 10 minutes

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1 Microsoft Clou

queries.spl:
index=windows
| bucket _time span=10m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 20
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where Count >= 20
| project TimeGenerated, UserId, ClientIP, Operation, OfficeWorkload
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 600 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_col_000062 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Mass File Download"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0009"
    technique = "T1213"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 10m

  condition:
    #event >= 20

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 10m)
| WHERE event_count >= 20
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 20)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=BootstrapProcess OR event_simpleName=SuspiciousFilesWithOperation
| where FileOperation IN ("WRITE","RENAME","DELETE")
| stats count(FileName) as file_count, dc(FileExtension) as ext_count by ComputerName, FileName_parent
| where file_count >= 50 AND ext_count >= 3
| sort -file_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 20
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count
queries.lucene:
source_name:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon AND event_id:11
AND NOT file_extension:(tmp OR log OR ini)

1 Salseforce Event  
Where the Event Type is 'WaveDownload'
Where the Source Username is NOT on the Salesforce Admins List

queries.spl:
index=windows
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_col_000417 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Unauthorized WaveDownload Event - Salesforce"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0009"
    technique = "T1213"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC

where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

queries.spl:
index=network sourcetype=proxy
| bucket _time span=1h
| stats count as requests, stdev(_time) as jitter, avg(bytes_out) as avg_bytes by src_ip, dest_host, _time
| where requests >= 10 AND jitter <= 300 AND avg_bytes < 5000
| table _time, src_ip, dest_host, requests, jitter, avg_bytes
queries.kql:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where DeviceAction == "allow"
| summarize Count = count(), Stdev = stdev(tolong(TimeGenerated)), AvgBytes = avg(SentBytes) by SourceIP, DestinationHostName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where Count >= 10 and Stdev <= 300 and AvgBytes < 5000
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, SUM(flowCount) AS total_flows
FROM flows
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule tde_c2_001 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Beaconing Detected - Periodic Outbound Connection"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0011"
    technique = "T1071.001"

  events:
    $network.metadata.event_type = "NETWORK_CONNECTION"
    $network.security_result.action = "ALLOW"
    $network.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $network.target.hostname = $dest_host
    $network.network.sent_bytes < 5000

  match:
    $src_ip, $dest_host over 1h

  condition:
    #network >= 10 and
    math.stddev(timestamp.seconds, $network) < 300

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(75)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-proxy*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.type == "allowed"
| STATS request_count = COUNT(*), avg_bytes = AVG(destination.bytes), std_time = STD_DEV(TO_LONG(@timestamp)) BY source.ip, destination.domain, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 1 hour)
| WHERE request_count >= 10 AND avg_bytes < 5000
| SORT request_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a web request to a script file that has not been requested before. New script files could be a web shell that was recently uploaded by an attacker. This building block will add the new script file names to a list to record them as observed and will be used for further correlation.
pseudo_logic:
1 Webserver Request Event
Where the URL Path ends with [.html, .htm, .php, .aspx, .asp, .jsp]
Where the Web Script File Name is NOT in the Observed Scripts List

queries.spl:
index=windows
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_c2_000233 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "New Webserver Scripts"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0011"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

rule zs_c2_000339 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "AWS DNS Request to DGA Domain - GuardDuty"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0011"
    technique = "T1568"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: APT User Agent
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects suspicious user agent strings used in APT malware in proxy logs
pseudo_logic:
1 Outbound Forward Proxy event
Where User Agent IN ('sjzj (compatible; msie 6.0; win32)', //APT Backspace
                          'mozilla/5.0 (windows nt 6.; wow64; rv:20.0) gecko/20100101 firefox/20.0', //APT GrizzlySteppe - ChopStick - US CERT
                          'user-agent: mozilla/4.0

queries.spl:
index=network
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (50727, 4322, 50727, 04506)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, SUM(flowCount) AS total_flows
FROM flows
WHERE eventid = 4322
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_c2_000430 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "APT User Agent"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0011"
    technique = "T1071"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4322"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-firewall*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4322])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4322
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4322)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: LSASS Memory Access by Non-System Process

queries.spl:
index=windows sourcetype="XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational" EventCode=10
| where TargetImage="C:\\Windows\\system32\\lsass.exe"
| where match(GrantedAccess, "0x1010|0x1410|0x1fffff")
| where NOT match(SourceImage, "(?i)(\\windows\\system32\\")
| table _time, host, SourceImage, GrantedAccess, CallTrace
queries.kql:
DeviceEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where ActionType == "OpenProcessApiCall"
| where FileName =~ "lsass.exe"
| where not(InitiatingProcessFolderPath startswith "C:\\Windows\\")
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceaddress AS source_host, username, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name,
       "Process Name" AS accessing_process, "Granted Access" AS access_mask
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 10
  AND "Target Image" ILIKE '%lsass.exe%'
  AND "Granted Access" IN ('0x1010','0x1410','0x1fffff','0x147a')
  AND "Source Image" NOT ILIKE '%\Windows\System32\%'
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 3600 SECONDS)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule tde_ca_001 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "LSASS Memory Access by Non-System Process"
    severity = "CRITICAL"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1003.001"

  events:
    $proc_access.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_OPEN"
    $proc_access.target.process.file.full_path = /lsass\.exe$/
    $proc_access.additional.fields["GrantedAccess"] = /0x1010|0x1410|0x1fffff/
    $proc_access.principal.process.file.full_path != /Windows\\System32/
    $proc_access.principal.hostname = $host

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #proc_access >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(95)
}

name: Account Guessing
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a potential account guessing attack against Windows accounts. Attackers may attempt to determine if a username exists by authenticating with a username and observing error messages received. The guessed usernames may be default accounts for applications or permutations the organization's naming convention. Once the attacker has determined if a username exists, they can target the existing

5 Windows Authentication Failure Events 
From the same internal Source IP to the same Destination

Where the Failure Code is "0xC0000064"
Where the Destination Username does NOT end with "$" 
Where the Destination IP/Hostname is NOT on the Domain Controller List

author: TDE/TDE

name: Wireless AP Broadcast Deauthentication
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a wireless deauthentication request that is sent to the broadcast MAC address of the wireless access point. The majority of wireless attacks on access points begin with the attacker capturing a client’s authentication. By sending a broadcast deauthentication packet, the access point will deauthenticate all clients on the network and an attacker can then capture their re-authentications.
pseudo_logic:
1 Wireless Access Point Event
Where the Event Name contains "deauthentication"
Where the Source Mac is "ff:ff:ff:ff:ff:ff"

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1 Wireless IDS/IPS Event
Where the Signature contains "Broadcast Deauthentication"

author: TDE/TDE

name: Windows Shadow Copy Creation
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when an attacker utilizes Windows shadow copy to copy the password hashes or system registry from a Domain Controller.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where Destination Host is a Domain Controller
Where Process Name contains ["vssadmin.exe" or "ntdsutil.exe" or "wmic.exe" or "powershell.exe"]
Where Command Line contains ["create" and "shadow"] or ["ntds" and "create"]

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, hostname, "Process Name" AS process, "Command Line" AS cmdline
FROM events
WHERE ("Process Name" ILIKE '%vssadmin.exe%' AND "Command Line" ILIKE '%delete%')
   OR ("Process Name" ILIKE '%wmic.exe%' AND "Command Line" ILIKE '%shadowcopy%delete%')
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 86400 SECONDS)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_ca_000115 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Windows Shadow Copy Creation"
    severity = "CRITICAL"
    tactic = "TA0040"
    technique = "T1490"

  events:
    $proc.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_LAUNCH"
    ($proc.target.process.file.full_path = /vssadmin\.exe$/
     and $proc.target.process.command_line = /delete/i)
    or
    ($proc.target.process.file.full_path = /wmic\.exe$/
     and $proc.target.process.command_line = /shadowcopy.*delete/i)
    $proc.principal.hostname = $host

  match:
    $host over 1h

  condition:
    #proc >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(100)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code == "4688"
| WHERE (process.name LIKE "*vssadmin.exe" AND process.command_line LIKE "*delete*")
     OR (process.name LIKE "*wmic.exe" AND process.command_line RLIKE "(?i)shadowcopy.*delete")
| KEEP host.name, user.name, process.name, process.command_line, @timestamp
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where((process CONTAINS "vssadmin.exe" AND command CONTAINS "delete")
  OR (process CONTAINS "wmic.exe" AND command CONTAINS "shadowcopy"))
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS vss_deletions)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| where (FileName MATCHES "(?i)vssadmin.exe" AND CommandLine MATCHES "(?i)delete")
    OR (FileName MATCHES "(?i)wmic.exe" AND CommandLine MATCHES "(?i)shadowcopy.*delete")
| table ComputerName, UserName, FileName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS"
| filter (action_process_image_name ~= "vssadmin.exe" and action_process_command_line contains "delete")
       or (action_process_image_name ~= "wmic.exe" and action_process_command_line ~= "(?i)shadowcopy.*delete")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time

tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects recent password changes throughout the environment and adds the use to a list that is excluded from brute force attacks. Hosts and other devices maybe slow to update passwords and could generate false positives. The source IP and username is added to a tracker list.
pseudo_logic:

Where the Event ID is in [628, 4724]

queries.spl:

name: Credential Dumping
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.

The lsass process is responsible for the authentication of users on a Windows host. Procdump is a tool that can dump a process’ memory to a file. The Task Manager can also be used to dump any process’ memory in the GUI. By dumping the memory of lsa
pseudo_logic:

Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where Destination Host is a Domain Controller
OR
    Where Process Name equals "reg.exe"
	OR
		Where Command Line contains "save hklm\system"
		Where Command Line contains "save hklm\sam"
		Where Command Line contains "save hklm\s

name: Windows Credential Editor Usage
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Windows Credential Editor is a password dumping tool.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Execution Event 
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where the Process Name in [wce.exe, wce32.exe, wce64.exe, getlsasrvaddr.exe, wce-universal.exe]

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where EventCode=4688
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID == 4688
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_ca_000226 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Windows Credential Editor Usage"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1003"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4688
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Count >= 25
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount

}

| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day

Where the Source IP/Hostname is NOT

author: TDE/TDE

name: AWS Brute Force Activity  - GuardDuty
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when brute force activity is detected within AWS instance sourcing from a remote source.
pseudo_logic:
1 Guard Duty Event 
Where the Event Type is in ["Impact:EC2/WinRMBruteForce", "UnauthorizedAccess:EC2/RDPBruteForce", "UnauthorizedAccess:EC2/SSHBruteForce"]

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_ca_000352 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "AWS Brute Force Activity  - GuardDuty"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1110"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 5m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

name: Mimikatz Module Use
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 30 days.
description: Detects the use of a known Mimikatz module. An attacker can use Mimikatz for credential extraction.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
AND CommandLine contains one of the following ['dumpcreds','mimikatz','sekurlsa::','rpc::','token::','crypto::','dpapi::','kerberos::','lsadump::','privilege::','process::','vault::']

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_ca_000425 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Mimikatz Module Use"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1003"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4688
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:

| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Golden Ticket Activity
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects when a source generates a ticket granting service (TGS) event without a ticket granting ticket (TGT) event, which means a Golden Ticket may be used within the environment. Adversaries who have the KRBTGT account password hash may forge Kerberos ticket-granting tickets (TGT), also known as a golden ticket. Using a golden ticket, adversaries are then able to request ticket granting service (
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Event
Where the Event ID is  "4769"
Where the Source user is NOT a machine account
Where the Service Name does NOT end with "$"
Where the Service Name is NOT "krbtgt"
With 5 Unique Service Names
From the Same Src IP and Src User
Within 10 Hours

AND where there are no 4768 Events within th

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4768 OR EventCode=4769 OR EventCode=4769 OR EventCode=4768)
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4768, 4769, 4769, 4768)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid IN (4768, 4769)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 36000 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_ca_000590 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Golden Ticket Activity"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1558"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4768"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 10h

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4768', '4769')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 10h)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4768, 4769])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4768 OR EventCode=4769
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4768, 4769)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")

queries.spl:
index=azure
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType

author: TDE/TDE

name: Windows Replay Attack Detected
false_positives: Miss-configuration may casuse false positives. Base line for atleast a week.
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: This rule identifies potential replay attacks on Windows systems. Replay attacks involve capturing and reusing valid authentication tokens or network traffic to gain unauthorized access. This detection focuses on anomalies such as repeated Kerberos authentication errors (e.g., KRB_AP_ERR_REPEAT ) or unusual patterns in authentication attempts.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Security Event
Where Event ID 4649

| where (EventCode=4649 OR EventCode=4649)

GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC

    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4649"

}

author: TDE/TDE

name: DSRM Persistence Usage
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects the usages of a DSRM account for persistence on a host. The DSRM account, being a local administrator account on the DC, is not subject to domain password policies. However, by default, its use is limited to specific conditions, such as when the DC is booted into DSRM. These limitations can be bypassed by altering the registry key responsible for the account’s logon behavior.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Security Event
Where the Event ID is 4657
Where the Key is HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Control\Lsa\DsrmAdminLogonBehavior
Where the New Value/Old Value is 1 OR 2

  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC

rule tde_de_001 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Windows Event Log Cleared"
    severity = "CRITICAL"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1070.001"

  events:
    $log_clear.metadata.event_type = "SYSTEM_AUDIT_LOG_WIPE"
    ($log_clear.metadata.product_event_type = "1102" or
     $log_clear.metadata.product_event_type = "104")
    $log_clear.principal.hostname = $host

  match:
    $host over 24h

  condition:

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(90)

| SORT @timestamp DESC

author: TDE/TDE

name: Audit Log Cleared
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description:
Detects when the Windows OS security log is cleared on a host. Attackers could attempt to erase their tracks by clearing logs that contains record of their malicious activity.

Detects when the Linux security log is cleared on a host. Attackers could attempt to erase their tracks by clearing logs that contains record of their malicious activity.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Audit Log Cleared Event
Where the Source is "Microsoft-Windows-Eventlog"
OR
	Where the Event Type is "Security"
	Where the Event ID is in [517, 1102]
	
	Where the Event Type is "System"
	Where the Event ID is "104"
	
Where the Destination Hostname is NOT in High Value Target Host List

queries.spl:
index=linux
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventid
FROM events
WHERE eventid IN (1102, 104)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 86400 SECONDS)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000032 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Audit Log Cleared"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1070.001"

  events:
    $log_clear.metadata.event_type = "SYSTEM_AUDIT_LOG_WIPE"
    ($log_clear.metadata.product_event_type = "1102" or
     $log_clear.metadata.product_event_type = "104")
    $log_clear.principal.hostname = $host

  match:
    $host over 24h

  condition:
    #log_clear >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(90)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-system.security*,logs-system.system*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("1102", "104")
| KEEP host.name, winlog.event_data.SubjectUserName, event.code, @timestamp
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [1102, 104])
groupby(hostname)
calculate(count AS clear_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityLogCleared OR event_simpleName=EventLogCleared
| table ComputerName, UserName, EventCode, ContextTimeStamp
| sort -ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter action_evtlog_event_id in (1102, 104)
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_evtlog_event_id, _time
| sort desc _time

name: Sensitive Registry Key Modification
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Windows log all registry keys that are modified on a system. If a registry key is modified that includes the file name detected by antivirus, there is evidence that execution may have taken place and the rule will create an event for correlation with other indicators of compromise.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Registry Modification Event
Where the Event ID is in [567, 4657]
Where the Object Name is on the Sensitive Registry Key List

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4657 OR EventCode=4657)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4657, 4657)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4657
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000153 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Sensitive Registry Key Modification"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1112"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4657"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}

author: TDE/TDE

name: File Encryption from Unsigned Process
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when an unsigned process executes an encryption process. Attackers or malicious processes may attempt to encrypt data on an infected host to either disguise data for exfiltration or damage the host by restricting access to core files, as used in ransomware.

queries.spl:
index=windows
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000217 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "File Encryption from Unsigned Process"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1036"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Typosquatting Activity
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects traffic to web domains that appear to be similar to a company's domain. Typosquatting can be used in targeted phishing attempts for credentials or to deploy malware. The list of domains is pulled by using DNS Twister
pseudo_logic:
2 URL Events
From the same Source
Where the Action is Allowed
Where the Destination Domain is on the Typosquatting Company Domains List
Within 5 minutes

queries.spl:
index=network
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 2
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Count >= 2
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, SUM(flowCount) AS total_flows
FROM flows
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000288 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Typosquatting Activity"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1583"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 2

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-firewall*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 2
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 2)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 2
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 2
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

name: Domain Added to Google Workspace Trusted Domains
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a domain is added to the list of trusted Google Workspace domains. An adversary may add a trusted domain in order to collect and exfiltrate data from their target’s organization with less restrictive security controls.
pseudo_logic:
1 Google Workspace Event
Where the events.name is "ADD_TRUSTED_DOMAINS"

queries.spl:
index=windows
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000302 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Domain Added to Google Workspace Trusted Domains"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1484"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Suspicious Conhost Child Process
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 30 days.
description: Detects Windows Console Host (conhost) spawning a child process. This is very unusual - investigate the parent/child relationships of the conhost process and determine why this is occurring.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where Parent Process Name equals "conhost.exe"
AND Process Name not in ["splwow64.exe", "werfault.exe", "conhost.exe"]

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000362 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Suspicious Conhost Child Process"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1202"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4688
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

name: PwnDrp Access
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects downloads from PwnDrp web servers developed for red team testing and most likely also used for criminal activity

queries.spl:
index=network
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, SUM(flowCount) AS total_flows
FROM flows
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000450 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "PwnDrp Access"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1071"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-firewall*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

1 Github event

| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action

SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount

    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)

| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:

| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")

author: TDE/TDE

name: Pass the Ticket Attack Activity
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects when a source generates ticket granting service (TGS) events without ticket granting ticket (TGT) events, which means a Pass the Ticket attack may be occurring. Adversaries may "pass the ticket" using stolen Kerberos tickets to move laterally within an environment, bypassing normal system access controls. Pass the ticket (PtT) is a method of authenticating to a system using Kerberos ticket
pseudo_logic:
5 Windows Events
Where the Event ID is  "4769"
Where the Source user is NOT a machine account
Where the Service Name does NOT end with "$"
Where the Service Name is NOT "krbtgt"
With 5 Unique Service Names
From the Same Src IP and Src User
Within 5 Hours

AND where there are no 4768 Events within th

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4768 OR EventCode=4769 OR EventCode=4769 OR EventCode=4768)
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 5
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4768, 4769, 4769, 4768)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Count >= 5
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid IN (4768, 4769)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 18000 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
HAVING event_count >= 5
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000589 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Pass the Ticket Attack Activity"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1550"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4768"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5h

  condition:
    #event >= 5

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4768', '4769')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5h)
| WHERE event_count >= 5
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4768, 4769])
groupby(source_ip, username, hostname)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 5)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4768 OR EventCode=4769
| stats count as events by ComputerName, UserName
| where events >= 5
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4768, 4769)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 5
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

author: TDE/TDE

name: Fork Bomb
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: This command indicates a Linux Fork Bomb. The fork bomb is a form of denial-of-service (DoS) attack against a Linux or Unix-based system. It makes use of the fork operation.
pseudo_logic:
1 command line event 
    where command line contains ":(){:|:&};:"

queries.spl:
index=linux
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000628 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Fork Bomb"
    severity = "MEDIUM"

author: TDE/TDE

name: Windows Active Directory Object WriteDAC Access
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects instances where WriteDAC permissions are modified or accessed. WriteDAC allows users to change the discretionary access control list (DACL) of an object, which defines the permissions for that object. Unauthorized or unexpected changes to WriteDAC permissions can indicate potential privilege escalation or malicious activity.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Security Event 
Where Event ID 4662
Where ObjectServer = "DS"
Where AccessMask = "0x40000"
OR
    Where ObjectType contains "19195a5b-6da0-11d0-afd3-00c04fd930c9"

    WhereObjectType contains "domainDNS"

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4662 OR EventCode=4662)
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4662, 4662)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4662
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000661 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Windows Active Directory Object WriteDAC Access"
    severity = "CRITICAL"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1222"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4662"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4662')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4662])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4662
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4662)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

name: Mass User Typosquatting Domain Access
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a mass amount of  web traffic from unqiue users to a web domain that appears to be similar to a company's domain. Typosquatting can be used in targeted phishing attempts for credentials or to deploy malware. The list of domains is pulled by using DNS Twister
pseudo_logic:
50 URL Events
With the same URL Domain
With uniue Usernames/IPs
Within 5 minutes

***Focus on using Usernames only if the log has the source user within it***

queries.spl:
index=network
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 50
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Count >= 50
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, SUM(flowCount) AS total_flows
FROM flows
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_de_000672 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Mass User Typosquatting Domain Access"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1583"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 50

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-firewall*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 50
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 50)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 50
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 50
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

author: TDE

name: Port Scan Detected - Internal
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a port scan from one internal host to another. Attackers will use port scans to perform reconnaissance to determine open services on the target.
pseudo_logic:
30 Firewall Events
From one internal source to one internal destination (Internal)
Where the Destination Port is 1-1024
Where the Destination Port is NOT in [53, 80 , 88, 129, 137, 138, 139, 161, 389, 443, 445]
OR
	Where the Log Source is Fortinet FortiGate
	Where the action is NOT in [server-rst, c

queries.spl:
index=network
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats dc(dest_port) as ports, dc(dest_ip) as hosts by _time, src_ip
| where ports >= 20 OR hosts >= 30
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
CommonSecurityLog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where Count >= 30
| project TimeGenerated, SourceIP, DestinationIP, DestinationPort, DeviceAction
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, COUNT(DISTINCT destinationport) AS unique_ports,
       COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) AS unique_hosts, COUNT(*) AS total_conns
FROM flows
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_ports >= 20 OR unique_hosts >= 30
ORDER BY unique_ports DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_dis_000033 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Port Scan Detected - Internal"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0007"
    technique = "T1046"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 10m

  condition:
    #event >= 30

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-firewall*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 hour
| STATS unique_ports = COUNT_DISTINCT(destination.port), unique_hosts = COUNT_DISTINCT(destination.ip), total = COUNT(*) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5 minutes)
| WHERE unique_ports >= 20 OR unique_hosts >= 30
| SORT unique_ports DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Firewall Activity")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_port AS unique_ports)
calculate(unique_count:destination_ip AS unique_hosts)
having(unique_ports >= 20 OR unique_hosts >= 30)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 30
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 30
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count
queries.lucene:
log_type:"firewall" AND NOT destination_ip:"10.0.0.0/8"
AND NOT destination_ip:"172.16.0.0/12"
AND NOT destination_ip:"192.168.0.0/16"

    author = "TDE"
    description = "Vulnerability Sweep - Internal"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0007"
    technique = "T1046"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 5

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-firewall*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 5
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 5)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 5
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 5
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

author: TDE/TDE

name: Unauthorized Access to Admin Share
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: A report that provides visibility into unauthorized users accessing the ADMIN$ share.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Network Share Accessed Event
Where the Event ID is in [5140, 5145]
Where the Share Name is "\\*\ADMIN$"
Where the Source Username is NOT in the Users Authorized to Access the Admin Share List
Where the Source Username does NOT end with "$"

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=5140 OR EventCode=5140 OR EventCode=5145)
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (5140, 5140, 5145)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_dis_000110 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Unauthorized Access to Admin Share"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0007"
    technique = "T1135"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: AWS User Enumeration
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a host executes AWS API calls that match enumeration tool behavior. Once an attacker obtains an API key for a user, they will attempt to enumerate the permissions of the user to determine which API calls they can use.
pseudo_logic:
5 AWS CloudTrail Events
From the same Source IP and Source Username
Where the Event Name is in [ListAccessKeys, ListUserPolicies, ListGroupsForUser, GetGroupPolicy, GetUser, ListQueues, DescribeDBSnapshots, DescribePermissions,GetAccountAuthorizationDetails,GetUserPolicy,ListAccessKeys,ListPolicies,

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| bucket _time span=1m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 5
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where Count >= 5
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 60 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC

tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a vulnerability scan from one internal host to an external host. Attackers will use vulnerability scans to perform reconnaissance to determine vulnerable services on the target. This could be  a compromised internal host attempting to scan other organizations’ networks for vulnerabilities in order to pivot.
pseudo_logic:
5 IDS/IPS Exploit Events 
From one internal source to one external destination (Outbound)
With unique Signatures 
Within 5 minutes

queries.spl:
index=windows
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 5
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action

SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, SUM(flowCount) AS total_flows

author: TDE/TDE

name: Webserver Directory Brute Force
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a host requesting multiple files that are not found on the web server and scanning tools used by attackers will attempt to enumerate common files hosted on the web server. However, the majroity of the time these files will not exist and return 404 errors.
pseudo_logic:
20 Webserver Request Events 
Where HTTP code matches "404"
With the Same Source IP
With unique URLs
within 2 minutes

queries.spl:
index=windows
| bucket _time span=2m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 20
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 2m)
| where Failures >= 20
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 120 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_dis_000237 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Webserver Directory Brute Force"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1083"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 2m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 2m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

name: Calls for List Buckets Potential Enumeration - GCP
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a service account listing buckets which may be an attempt to enumerate the list of buckets within an instance. Service accounts typically do not need to list storage buckets because they are already configured to access the buckets they need.
pseudo_logic:
1 Cloud Audit event
Where message.data.protoPayload.methodName contains storage.buckets.list

queries.spl:
index=windows
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_dis_000319 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Calls for List Buckets Potential Enumeration - GCP"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0007"
    technique = "T1083"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC

where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:

| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Advanced IP/Port Scanner Update Check
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detect update check performed by Advanced IP Scanner and Advanced Port Scanner
pseudo_logic:
1 Outbound Forward Proxy event
Where URL contains "checkupdate.php"
    AND Where Query Parameters Contain ("lng="
    OR "ver="
    OR "beta="
    OR "type="
    OR "rmode="
    OR "product=")

queries.spl:
index=network
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats dc(dest_port) as ports, dc(dest_ip) as hosts by _time, src_ip
| where ports >= 20 OR hosts >= 30
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, COUNT(DISTINCT destinationport) AS unique_ports,
       COUNT(DISTINCT destinationip) AS unique_hosts, COUNT(*) AS total_conns
FROM flows
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_ports >= 20 OR unique_hosts >= 30
ORDER BY unique_ports DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_dis_000429 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Advanced IP/Port Scanner Update Check"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0007"
    technique = "T1590"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"

author: TDE/TDE

name: Persistent Malware
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a specific piece of malware that is repeatedly detected and cleaned by the AV on a system. This could indicate that the AV is leaving remnants of the malware when deleting it that cause it to be installed again after it has been cleaned. This could also be an indication that a user has bad network practice and is repeatedly infected with the same malware.
pseudo_logic:
4 Malware Detection Events
With the same Source Hostname and File Hash/Signature
Within 20 minutes

queries.spl:
index=windows
| bucket _time span=20m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 4
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 20m)
| where Count >= 4
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 1200 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC

  meta:
