    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1204"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 20m

  condition:
    #event >= 4

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 20m)
| WHERE event_count >= 4
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 4)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 4
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:

| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")

tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.

Where the Event ID is in [602, 4698]
Where the Source Username does NOT end with "$"
Where the Destination Hostname is on the High Value Target Host List

| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity

  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC

    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4698"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}

name: Service Installation - HVT
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a new service installed on a HVT host. Attackers will commonly establish persistent access to a compromised host by installing a new service to run their code. HVT hosts should not typically have frequent service installations, so any instances should be considered suspicious. The alert will be sent as a report for the first 30 days then moved to an alert.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Service Installed Event
Where the Event ID is in [601, 4697]
Where the Source Username does NOT end with "$"
Where the Destination Hostname is on the High Value Target Host List

  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC

name: PSEXEC Detection
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects an attacker pivoting internally using PSEXEC. PSEXEC is a common administrator tool used to remotely execute commands and manage systems. PSEXEC connects to the ADMIN$ share of an internal host and then immediately installs a windows service image. This rule is designed to detect this type of activity.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Execution Event 
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where the Process Name is psexec.exe

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1 Windows Network Share Object Accessed Event
Where the Event ID is in [5140,

| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 5140, 4697, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events

GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC

author: TDE/TDE

name: Abnormal LSASS Parent Child Relationship
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 14 days.
description: Detects potential lsass.exe abuse based on unusual and suspicious parent-child relationships. Lsass doesn’t typically spawn additional processes. This also detects suspicious parent processes of lsass, which may indicate LSASS memory access attempts.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where Parent Process Name equals "lsass.exe"
AND Process Name in ["cmd.exe", "powershel.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "mstsc.exe", "dllhost.exe"]
OR Process Name equals "lsass.exe"
AND Parent Process Name in ["explorer.exe", "cmd.exe", "l

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceaddress AS source_host, username, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name,
       "Process Name" AS accessing_process, "Granted Access" AS access_mask
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 10
  AND "Target Image" ILIKE '%lsass.exe%'
  AND "Granted Access" IN ('0x1010','0x1410','0x1fffff','0x147a')
  AND "Source Image" NOT ILIKE '%\Windows\System32\%'
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 3600 SECONDS)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000200 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Abnormal LSASS Parent Child Relationship"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1003.001"

  events:
    $proc_access.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_OPEN"
    $proc_access.target.process.file.full_path = /lsass\.exe$/
    $proc_access.additional.fields["GrantedAccess"] = /0x1010|0x1410|0x1fffff/
    $proc_access.principal.process.file.full_path != /Windows\\System32/
    $proc_access.principal.hostname = $host

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #proc_access >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(95)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code == "10"
| WHERE winlog.event_data.TargetImage LIKE "*\\lsass.exe"
| WHERE winlog.event_data.GrantedAccess IN ("0x1010","0x1410","0x1fffff","0x147a")
| WHERE NOT winlog.event_data.SourceImage LIKE "*\\Windows\\System32\\*"
| KEEP host.name, winlog.event_data.SourceImage, winlog.event_data.GrantedAccess, winlog.event_data.CallTrace, @timestamp
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(source_name = "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
  AND event_id = 10
  AND target_image CONTAINS "lsass.exe"
  AND granted_access IN ["0x1010","0x1410","0x1fffff"]
  AND NOT source_image CONTAINS "Windows\System32")
calculate(count AS total)
having(total >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| where TargetProcessName MATCHES "(?i)lsass.exe"
| where DesiredAccess IN ("0x1010","0x1410","0x1fffff")
| where FileName NOT MATCHES "(?i)C:\\Windows\\System32"
| table ComputerName, FileName, TargetProcessName, DesiredAccess, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "INJECTION"
| filter action_remote_process_image_path contains "lsass.exe"
| filter actor_process_image_path not contains "C:\Windows\System32"
| fields hostname, actor_process_image_name, action_remote_process_image_path, actor_effective_username, _time
| sort desc _time
queries.lucene:
event_id:10 AND target_image:*lsass.exe
AND granted_access:(0x1010 OR 0x1410 OR 0x1fffff)
AND NOT source_image:*Windows\System32*

author: TDE/TDE

name: Vulnerable Process Launching Shell
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a browser or applications associated with a browser launches a script or shell process on the target host. Browsers and other applications such as Java or Flash are commonly targeted by attackers hosting malicious web sites. Malicious scripts on the site will automatically exploit vulnerable versions of the software and allow the attacker to execute code or drop files on the host.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Execution Event 
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where the Parent Process Name is in [firefox.exe, chrome.exe, microsoftedge.exe, safari.exe, opera.exe, iexplore.exe, vivaldi.exe, brave.exe, java.exe, flash.exe]
Where the Process Name ends with [.BAT,.BIN,.CMD,.COM,.CPL,.GADGE

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000232 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Vulnerable Process Launching Shell"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1546"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:

author: TDE/TDE

name: Recon Commands
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects an attacker that may have compromised an internal host and is now running commands via command prompt or PowerShell in an attempt to map the network. This activity has been made as a building block instead of an alert to account for legitimate users that may run one off recon commands.
pseudo_logic:
2 or more Windows Process Executions
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where the Command contains one of [whoami, ipconfig, nestat, tasklist, ver, systeminfo, net time, net start, qprocess, query, dir, net view, ping, net use, type, net user, net localgroup, net group, net config, net share]

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 2
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Count >= 2
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
HAVING event_count >= 2
ORDER BY starttime DESC

    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"

    $risk_score = max(50)

name: PowerShell Remote Execution
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: PowerShell can be used to execute commands on remote computers using WinRM over WMI. Attackers can use this as an alternative to PSEXEC tools to pivot and maintain access to compromised hosts. Alert will be sent in a weekly report only for the first 30 days from implementation.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Execution Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
OR

	Where the Process Name is wsmprovhost.exe

NOT (Where the Process Name is "DismHost.exe" AND the Process Path starts with "C:\Windows\Temp\")

author: TDE/TDE

name: Traffic Pattern to Randomly Generated Domain
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a single ip performs multiple connections to a randomly generated domain. Botnets commonly use Domain Generation Algorithms, DGA, to generate and register domains for Command and Control purposes. This could be an indication that the impacted host is infected.
pseudo_logic:
5 Forward Proxy Events
From one internal source to on external destination (Outbound)
Where the Destination Domain matches the DGA Domain Regex
With the same Destination Domain
Within 3 minutes

queries.spl:
index=network
| bucket _time span=3m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 5
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where Count >= 5
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT sourceip, destinationip, destinationport, SUM(flowCount) AS total_flows
FROM flows
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip, destinationip, destinationport
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000254 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Traffic Pattern to Randomly Generated Domain"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1568"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 3m

  condition:
    #event >= 5

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-network*,logs-firewall*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE event_count >= 5
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 5)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 5
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "NETWORK" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 5
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

author: TDE/TDE

name: Command Executed Within Kube-System Namespace - GuardDuty
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a command is executed within the kube-system namespace. The kube-system namespace is a default namespace, which is primarily used for system level components such as kube-dns and kube-proxy. It is very uncommon to execute commands inside pods or containers under kube-system namespace and may indicate suspicious activity.
pseudo_logic:
1 Guard Duty Event 
Where the Event Type is in ["Execution:Kubernetes/ExecInKubeSystemPod"]

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000347 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Command Executed Within Kube-System Namespace - GuardDuty"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1609"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Cobalt Strike Process Patterns
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 14 days.
description: Detects process patterns found in Cobalt Strike beacon activity. This includes the use of named pipes to communicate the result of post-exploitation tools to the Cobalt Strike beacon.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where
	Parent Process Name starts with "C:\Temp"
	AND Command Line contains "cmd.exe /c whoami"
OR
	Process Name is dllhost.exe
	AND Command Line contains any of ["cmd.exe /c echo", "> \\.\pipe", "whoami.exe"]
OR
	Parent Process N

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000359 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Cobalt Strike Process Patterns"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1059"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4688
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Encoded Powershell
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 30 days.
description: Detects powershell with suspicious encoding options. Attackers frequently use base64 and similar encoding to evade detection. Investigate parent/child relationships to determine whether the activity is authorized.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where Process Name equals "powershell.exe"
AND Command Line contains ".*(-ec|-en|-enc|\/ec|\/-en|\/-enc|frombase64string|-EncodedCommand|base64)\b.*"

author: TDE/TDE

name: AWS CLI Session from Unauthorized Country
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 14 days.
description: Detects the use of the AWS CLI from an abnormal country.
pseudo_logic:
1 AWS CloudTrail Event
Where the Event Type is in [AwsApiCall]
Where the Event Name is in [StartSession]
Where the Source Country is a high risk or unauthorized country

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000372 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "AWS CLI Session from Unauthorized Country"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1651"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Possible Impacket Lateral Movement
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 14 days.
description: Detects commands from the Impacket framework where an attacker will attempt to use an semi-interactive shell via WMI or SMB to execute commands for lateral movement.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where Parent Process Name contains "wmiprvse.exe" or Parent Process Name contains "services.exe"
AND CommandLine contains 'cmd.exe' and '/q' and '\\\\127.0.0.1\\' and '&1'

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000424 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Possible Impacket Lateral Movement"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1047"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogon
| where LogonType = 3 AND AuthenticationPackage = "NTLM" AND UserName!="*$"
| stats dc(RemoteIP) as src_count by UserName, aid
| where src_count >= 3
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: ICMP Exfiltration Commands
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects a PowerShell script or command containing ICMP exfiltration commands. Derived from a Sigma rule created by "Bartlomiej Czyz (@bczyz1)" and "oscd.community".
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Execution Event 
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where the Process Name is powershell.exe
Where Command contains "New-Object"
Where Command contains "System.Net.NetworkInformation.Ping"
Where Command contains ".Send("

----------------------------------------------------------

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4104 OR EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4104)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4104, 4688, 4104)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid IN (4688, 4104)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_exe_000516 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "ICMP Exfiltration Commands"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0002"
    technique = "T1204"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688', '4104')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688, 4104])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4104
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688, 4104)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

name: Kubernetes Pod Created with hostNetwork

Where the Source Username is NOT "system:serviceaccount:kube-system:replicaset-controller"
Where the Source Username is NOT "system:serviceaccount:kube-system:daemon-set-control

pseudo_logic:

queries.spl:

| where match(dest_host, "(?i)(mega\.nz|dropbox\.com|box\.com|wetransfer\.com)")

author: TDE

name: Shadow Copy Deletion via vssadmin or wmic
false_positives: Authorized backup solutions managing shadow storage

queries.spl:
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4688
| where (ProcessName="vssadmin.exe" AND match(CommandLine,"(?i)delete"))
   OR (ProcessName="wmic.exe" AND match(CommandLine,"(?i)(shadowcopy.*delete)"))
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
DeviceProcessEvents
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)delete")
    or (FileName =~ "wmic.exe" and ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)(shadowcopy.*delete)")
| project TimeGenerated, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine
queries.aql:
SELECT username, hostname, "Process Name" AS process, "Command Line" AS cmdline
FROM events
WHERE ("Process Name" ILIKE '%vssadmin.exe%' AND "Command Line" ILIKE '%delete%')
   OR ("Process Name" ILIKE '%wmic.exe%' AND "Command Line" ILIKE '%shadowcopy%delete%')
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 86400 SECONDS)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule tde_imp_002 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Shadow Copy Deletion via vssadmin or wmic"
    severity = "CRITICAL"
    tactic = "TA0040"
    technique = "T1490"

  events:
    $proc.metadata.event_type = "PROCESS_LAUNCH"
    ($proc.target.process.file.full_path = /vssadmin\.exe$/
     and $proc.target.process.command_line = /delete/i)
    or
    ($proc.target.process.file.full_path = /wmic\.exe$/
     and $proc.target.process.command_line = /shadowcopy.*delete/i)
    $proc.principal.hostname = $host

  match:
    $host over 1h

  condition:
    #proc >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(100)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code == "4688"
| WHERE (process.name LIKE "*vssadmin.exe" AND process.command_line LIKE "*delete*")
     OR (process.name LIKE "*wmic.exe" AND process.command_line RLIKE "(?i)shadowcopy.*delete")
| KEEP host.name, user.name, process.name, process.command_line, @timestamp
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where((process CONTAINS "vssadmin.exe" AND command CONTAINS "delete")
  OR (process CONTAINS "wmic.exe" AND command CONTAINS "shadowcopy"))
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS vss_deletions)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| where (FileName MATCHES "(?i)vssadmin.exe" AND CommandLine MATCHES "(?i)delete")
    OR (FileName MATCHES "(?i)wmic.exe" AND CommandLine MATCHES "(?i)shadowcopy.*delete")
| table ComputerName, UserName, FileName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS"
| filter (action_process_image_name ~= "vssadmin.exe" and action_process_command_line contains "delete")
       or (action_process_image_name ~= "wmic.exe" and action_process_command_line ~= "(?i)shadowcopy.*delete")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time

name: DHCP Starvation
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: An attacker can broadcast DHCPDISCOVER messages with spoofed MAC addresses to force the DHCP server to lease all of the IPs in the address pool this can cause a denial-of-service.
pseudo_logic:
200 DHCP Events
Where the Event Type is DHCPACK
Where the Destination IP (Allocated IP) is in the DHCP IP Range
With unique Source MAC Addresses
Within 1 minute

| where count >= 200
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action

name: Default Account Logon
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a successful logon to a default account. Attackers may attempt to authenticate to a default account in hopes that the account is still active.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Authentication Success Event
Where the Event ID is in [528, 540, 4624]
From an internal Source IP 
OR
	Where the Destination Username is case insensitive "Guest" OR "Administrator"
			
	Where the Destination Username is in Customer Default Account List

Where the source is NOT on the Domai

queries.spl:
index=linux
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4624
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000001 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Default Account Logon"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4624"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-system.syslog*,logs-auditd*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4624')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC

author: TDE/TDE

name: Successful Brute Force
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a successful login after the IP or host has been identified by password guessing. This could indicate that an attacker was successful in guessing a user’s password and has compromised their account.
pseudo_logic:
1 Password Guessing Event (5 failures within 3 minutes)

Followed By

1 Windows Authentication Success Event
Where the Event ID is in [528, 540, 4624]
With the same Source IP/Hostname
With the same Destination Username
With the same Destination Host
	
Within 5 minutes

queries.spl:
index=linux
| bucket _time span=3m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000009 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Successful Brute Force"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 3m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-system.syslog*,logs-auditd*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

name: Service Account Login with Cleartext Password
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a logon to a Windows service account where the password was sent in clear text. This identifies when the service account is authenticating using an insecure method.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Authentication Success Event
Where the Event ID is in [528, 540, 4624]
Where the Logon Type is 8
OR
	Where the Destination Username is on the Service Account List
	Where the Destination Username matches the Service Account Naming Convention
Where Logon Process is NOT [Advapi]

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4624 OR EventCode=4624)
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4624, 4624)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity

SELECT username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventid
FROM events
WHERE eventid IN (1102, 104)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 86400 SECONDS)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000011 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Service Account Login with Cleartext Password"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0005"
    technique = "T1070.001"

  events:
    $log_clear.metadata.event_type = "SYSTEM_AUDIT_LOG_WIPE"
    ($log_clear.metadata.product_event_type = "1102" or
     $log_clear.metadata.product_event_type = "104")
    $log_clear.principal.hostname = $host

  match:
    $host over 24h

  condition:
    #log_clear >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(90)
}

FROM logs-system.security*,logs-system.system*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("1102", "104")
| KEEP host.name, winlog.event_data.SubjectUserName, event.code, @timestamp
| SORT @timestamp DESC

groupby(hostname)
calculate(count AS clear_count)

author: TDE/TDE

name: Successful Remote Brute Force
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: This rule is designed to detect a login success from a source that has been previously flagged for Remote Password Guessing, this may indicate an attacker successfully guessed a users password.
pseudo_logic:
10 Remote Authentication Failure events
From 1 external source to the same destination
With the same Source IP and Destination Username
Within 3 minutes

Followed By

1 Successful Remote Login Event
With the same Source IP and Destination Username from the previous event
Within 15 minutes


--------

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| bucket _time span=3m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Location, ResultType
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000031 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Successful Remote Brute Force"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 3m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000044 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Remote Password Spraying"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 3m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

author: TDE/TDE

name: Remote Password Guessing
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: This rule is designed to detect multiple failed authentication attempts against a VPN or public-facing host, which may indicate an attempt to brute force an account’s password.
pseudo_logic:
75 Remote Authentication Failure events
From 1 external source to the same destination
With the same Destination Username
Within 3 minutes

queries.spl:
index=azure
| bucket _time span=3m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 75
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where Count >= 75
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Location, ResultType
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000045 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Remote Password Guessing"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 3m

  condition:
    #event >= 75

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE event_count >= 75
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 75)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 75
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 75
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

name: Successful Click Permitted from Multiple Users
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when multiple users have "clicked" or interacted with an e-mail that has been classified malicious. Attackers will typically send phishing e-mails to users in an attempt to gain access to the organization's network.
pseudo_logic:
10 URL Click Permitted Events
With the same URL
With unique Source Usernames
Within 8 hours

queries.spl:
index=windows
| bucket _time span=8m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 10
| table _time, host, user, _raw
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 8m)
| where Count >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 28800 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000087 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Successful Click Permitted from Multiple Users"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1566"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 8h

  condition:
    #event >= 10

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 8h)
| WHERE event_count >= 10
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 10)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 10
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 10
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.

1 AWS CloudTrail Event
Where the Event Type is AwsApiCall
Where the User Identity is IAMUser
Where the Source IP is on a Threat Intelligence List

| table _time, host, user, _raw

AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity

    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)

queries.leql:

	Where the Application is case insensitive "RDP"
	
Where the Destination IP is on a Threat Intelligence List

    #event >= 1

    $risk_score = max(50)
}

author: TDE/TDE

name: HVT User Account Lockout
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a high value target account is locked out. This is indicative of a potential brute force attack targeting the account.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Account Locked Out Event
Where the Event ID is in [644, 4740]
Where the Destination Username is in the High Value Target Users List

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

1 User Locked Out Event
Where the Destinatio

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4740 OR EventCode=4740)
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4740, 4740)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4740
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000111 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "HVT User Account Lockout"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1531"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4740"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4740')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1

author: TDE/TDE

name: AWS Password Spraying
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects an attacker attempting to guess the passwords of multiple AWS users that may not exist in AWS. An attacker may use default credentials against known accounts or attempt to guess other accounts based on naming conventions to gain access to the AWS console.
pseudo_logic:
5 AWS CloudTrail Events
From the same Source IP
Where the Event ID is "ConsoleLogin"
Where the User Type (userIdentity.type) is "IAMUser"
Where the Result (responseElements.ConsoleLogin) is "Failure"
With unique Destination Usernames
Within 5 minutes

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000129 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "AWS Password Spraying"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 5m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

author: TDE/TDE

name: AWS Unauthorized Action Attempts
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects an AWS account that has been blocked from performing multiple actions due to security permissions. An attacker with a compromised account may not know the account’s permissions and will attempt abusive activities, which may be blocked due to insufficient privileges.
pseudo_logic:
3 AWS CloudTrail events
Where the Event Type is AwsApiCall
Where the User Type (userIdentity.type) is "IAMUser"
Where the Error_code is in [AccessDenied, UnauthorizedOperation]
Within 1 minute

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| bucket _time span=1m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 3
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where Count >= 3
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 60 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000133 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "AWS Unauthorized Action Attempts"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1098"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 1m

  condition:
    #event >= 3

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 1m)
| WHERE event_count >= 3
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 3
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 3
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

name: AWS Privilege Escalation via Role Policy
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects an attacker escalating the privileges of an AWS role. If the attacker has access to the role, the attacker will then have escalated privileges.
pseudo_logic:
1 AWS APICall Event
Where the Event Name is in [AttachRolePolicy, PutRolePolicy]
Where Policy ARN is Client HVT Policy
OR 
Where the Policy ARN is in [arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AdministratorAccess" or "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonSNSFullAccess" or "arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/AmazonEC2FullAccess" or "ar

  meta:

}

author: TDE/TDE

name: Webserver Command Injection Attempt
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a host attempting to call the command line on a web server to execute remote commands and if the web server is vulnerable to injection, an attacker can pipe commands into CMD that will be processed by the server.
pseudo_logic:
1 Webserver Request Event
Where the HTTP code >= 200
Where the HTTP code < 400
OR

	Where URL contains "cmd.exe"
	
	Where URL contains "xp_cmdshell"
	
	Where URL contains "=whoami"
	
	Where URL contains "=net%20user"
	
	Where URL contains "=net+user"
	
	Where URL contains "=net%2buser"
	
	Where URL

queries.spl:
index=windows
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, dest_port, action
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000236 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Webserver Command Injection Attempt"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1202"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE/TDE

name: Repeat Account Lockout
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Designed to detect multiple account lockout events for a user in a 60 minute period. Investigate the source of the failed logins as well as any cause of the failed logins (wrong password, process logging in)
pseudo_logic:
5 Windows Account Locked Out Events
Where the Event ID is in [644, 4740]
With the same Destination Username
Within 1 hour

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

5 User Account Locked Out Events
With the same Destination Usern

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4740 OR EventCode=4740)
| bucket _time span=1m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 5
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4740, 4740)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where Count >= 5
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4740
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 3600 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
HAVING event_count >= 5
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000270 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Repeat Account Lockout"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1531"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4740"

author: TDE/TDE

name: Stealth Password Spraying
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a potential low and slow password spraying attack against Windows accounts. Attackers may have gathered a list of user accounts and could be attempting to use common passwords against each one in order to compromise the accounts. The attacker, in this instance, is preforming the spray at a slow rate to avoid detection and prevent lockouts.
pseudo_logic:
4 Instances over 1 hour of at least 2 Windows Authentication Failure Events 
From the same internal Source IP to the same Destination
Where the Event ID in [529, 4625]
Where the Failure Code is "0xC000006A"
Where the Destination Username does NOT end with "$" 
Where the Destination IP/Hostname is NO

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4625 OR EventCode=4625)
| bucket _time span=1m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4625, 4625)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 3600 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000276 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Stealth Password Spraying"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 1h

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 1h)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

name: Stealth Remote Password Spraying
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: This rule is designed to detect a low and slow password spray attempt against a VPN or public-facing host. This could indicate an attacker attempting a single password across multiple accounts
pseudo_logic:
4 Instances over 1 hour of 2 Remote Login Failures
From the same Source
With unique Destination Usernames
Within 3 minutes

queries.spl:
index=azure
| bucket _time span=1m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Location, ResultType
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events

author: TDE/TDE

name: AWS Successful RDS Login Brute Force - GuardDuty
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects a successful brute force against a RDS database
pseudo_logic:
1 Guard Duty Event 
Where the Event Type is in ["CredentialAccess:RDS/AnomalousBehavior.SuccessfulBruteForce"]

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000393 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "AWS Successful RDS Login Brute Force - GuardDuty"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 5m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-aws.cloudtrail*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

author: TDE/TDE

name: Spike in Failed Remote Login Attempts for a User
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 30 days.
description: Detects a spike in failed remote logins for a single username
pseudo_logic:
Failed Remote Login Events
Where the Source IP is an External IP

Baseline Type: Daily
Entity: Username

Baseline Learning: 30 Days
Retention: 90 Days

Outlier: count
Sensitivity: 2
Minimum: 10

queries.spl:
index=azure
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Location, ResultType
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 120 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000402 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Spike in Failed Remote Login Attempts for a User"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 2m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 2m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

queries.crowdstrike:

author: TDE/TDE

name: Domain Password Spray Commandlet Activity
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: Detects when a PowerShell script or command line contains the commandlets to perform a password spray.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Execution Event 
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where the Process Name is powershell.exe
OR
    Where the Command Line contains "Invoke-DomainPasswordSpray"
    Where the Command Line contains "Get-DomainUserList"
    Where the Command Line contains "Invoke-SpraySinglePasswor

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4104 OR EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4104)
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4104, 4688, 4104)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000514 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Domain Password Spray Commandlet Activity"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1204"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 5m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

author: TDE/TDE

name: HVT Remote Password Guessing
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: This rule is designed to detect multiple failed authentication attempts against a VPN or public-facing host, which may indicate an attempt to brute force an account’s password.
pseudo_logic:
75 Remote Authentication Failure events
From 1 external source to the same destination
With the same Destination Username
Within 3 minutes

queries.spl:
index=azure
| bucket _time span=3m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 75
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SigninLogs
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where Count >= 75
| project TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, IPAddress, Location, ResultType
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000527 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "HVT Remote Password Guessing"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 3m

  condition:
    #event >= 75

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE event_count >= 75
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 75)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 75
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 75
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count

name: MFA Pin Guessing
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: This rule is designed to detect multiple failed MFA pin code attempts for the same user against a company's MFA application. This may indicate an attempt to brute force a user's account and bypass MFA
pseudo_logic:
5 MFA Login Failures
From the same external  Source
Where the MFA Factor is a Pin or Device Code
Where the Destination Username is NOT on the High Value Target User List
With the same Destination Username
Within 3 minutes

queries.spl:
index=azure
| bucket _time span=3m
| stats count by src_ip, Account_Name, _time
| where count >= 5
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
OktaLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where Count >= 5
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000592 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "MFA Pin Guessing"
    severity = "LOW"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 3m

  condition:
    #event >= 5

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE event_count >= 5
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 5)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 5
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as event_count by hostname, actor_effective_username
| filter event_count >= 5
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, event_count
