author: TDE/TDE

name: Successful MFA Pin Brute Force
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description: This rule is designed to detect multiple failed MFA pin code attempts for the same user against a company's MFA application followed by a successful login via a MFA pin code. This may indicate a successful brute force attempt against a user's account
pseudo_logic:
5 MFA Failure events
From an external source
Where the MFA Factor is a Pin or Device Code
With the same Source IP and Destination Username

Followed By

1 Successful MFA Login Event
From an external source
Where the MFA Factor is a Pin or Device Code
With the same Source IP and Destination Username

queries.spl:
index=azure
| bucket _time span=10m
| stats count as failures, dc(Account_Name) as unique_users by _time, IpAddress
| where failures >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
OktaLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Failures = count(), UniqueAccounts = dcount(TargetUserName) by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 10m)
| where Failures >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT username, sourceip, COUNT(*) AS failure_count, UNIQUECOUNT(username) AS unique_accounts
FROM events
WHERE LOGSOURCETYPENAME(devicetype) ILIKE '%Windows%'
  AND eventid IN (4625, 529, 4776)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 600 SECONDS)
GROUP BY sourceip
HAVING unique_accounts >= 10 AND (failure_count / unique_accounts) <= 3
ORDER BY failure_count DESC
START '2026-04-27 00:00:00'
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000594 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Successful MFA Pin Brute Force"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0006"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $login.metadata.event_type = "USER_LOGIN"
    $login.metadata.product_name = "Windows"
    $login.security_result.action = "BLOCK"
    $login.principal.ip = $src_ip
    $login.target.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $src_ip over 10m

  condition:
    #login > 10 and
    #user > 8

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ("4625", "529")
| STATS failure_count = COUNT(*), unique_accounts = COUNT_DISTINCT(winlog.event_data.TargetUserName) BY source.ip, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 10m)
| WHERE unique_accounts >= 10
| EVAL ratio = failure_count / unique_accounts
| WHERE ratio <= 3
| SORT failure_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4625, 529] AND logset = "Active Directory")
groupby(source_ip)
calculate(unique_count:destination_account AS unique_accounts)
calculate(count AS total_failures)
having(unique_accounts >= 10)
having(total_failures / unique_accounts <= 3)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogonFailed2
| stats count(TargetUserName) as failures, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_accounts by RemoteIP
| where unique_accounts >= 10 AND failures/unique_accounts <= 3
| sort -unique_accounts
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" and action_evtlog_event_id = 4625
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| comp count() as failure_count, dc(actor_effective_username) as unique_accounts by src_ip
| filter unique_accounts >= 10
| fields src_ip, failure_count, unique_accounts
queries.lucene:
event_id:(4625 OR 529) AND log_source_type:"Windows Security"
AND NOT user_name:("ANONYMOUS LOGON" OR "*$")

author: TDE/TDE

name: Logon with Break Glass Account
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: A rule that detects when there is a successful logon into a break glass account. Break glass accounts are highly privileged accounts and should only be used in emergency situations.
pseudo_logic:
1 Successful Sign-In Event
Where the source user is in Break Glass Account list

queries.spl:
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
AWSCloudTrail
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_auth_000615 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Logon with Break Glass Account"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0001"
    technique = "T1078"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-azure*,logs-o365*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = cloud_audit_log
| filter event_type = "CLOUD" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

name: Pass-the-Hash NTLM Lateral Movement
false_positives: Legitimate admin accounts managing multiple systems

queries.spl:
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624
| where Logon_Type=3 AND Authentication_Package="NTLM"
| where NOT match(Account_Name,"\\$$")
| bucket _time span=1h
| stats dc(IpAddress) as src_ips by Account_Name, _time
| where src_ips >= 3
| table _time, Account_Name, src_ips
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID == 4624 and LogonType == 3 and AuthenticationPackageName == "NTLM"
| where TargetUserName !endswith "$"
| summarize SourceIPs = dcount(IpAddress) by TargetUserName, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where SourceIPs >= 3
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4624
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule tde_lm_001 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Pass-the-Hash NTLM Lateral Movement"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0008"
    technique = "T1550.002"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4624"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4624')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4624])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=UserLogon
| where LogonType = 3 AND AuthenticationPackage = "NTLM" AND UserName!="*$"
| stats dc(RemoteIP) as src_count by UserName, aid
| where src_count >= 3
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4624)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

author: TDE

name: Domain Account Deleted Within 24 Hours of Creation
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: Detects when a domain account is created and deleted within a 24 hour period.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows User Created Event
Where the Event ID is in [624, 4720]
Where the Destination User Domain is in the Company AD Domains List

Followed By

1 Windows User Deleted Event
Where the Event ID is in [630, 4726]
With the same Destination Username from the previous event	
Within 24 hours

queries.spl:
index=linux
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid IN (4720, 4726)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 86400 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_per_000109 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Domain Account Deleted Within 24 Hours of Creation"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0003"
    technique = "T1136"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4720"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 24h

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-system.syslog*,logs-auditd*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4720', '4726')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 24h)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4720, 4726])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:

queries.xql:

author: TDE/TDE

name: Mass Group Deletions
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: This rule is designed to alert when several user groups are removed by the same user. Several groups being deleted by same user in a short amount of time could indicate malicious activity.
pseudo_logic:
10 Windows Group Deleted Events
Where the Event ID is in [634, 4730, 638, 4734, 662, 4758]
From a single Source Username
Where the Source Username is NOT on the Users Authorized to Delete Users and Groups List
Within 3 minutes

------------------------------------------------------------------------

queries.spl:
index=linux
| bucket _time span=5m
| stats count as total, dc(TargetUserName) as unique_targets by _time, SubjectUserName
| where total >= 10
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
Syslog
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| summarize Count = count() by Account, IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 3m)
| where Count >= 10
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid IN (4730, 4734, 4758)
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 180 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)
HAVING event_count >= 10
ORDER BY starttime DESC

  meta:
    author = "TDE"

    tactic = "TA0003"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4730"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 3m

  condition:
    #event >= 10

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-system.syslog*,logs-auditd*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4730', '4734', '4758')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 3m)
| WHERE event_count >= 10
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4730, 4734, 4758])
groupby(source_ip, username, hostname)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 10)
queries.crowdstrike:

| where events >= 10
queries.xql:

name: User Password Set to Never Expire
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 7 days.
description: A rule that detects when an end user's password is set to never expire.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows User Account Change Event
Where the Event ID is in [4738]
Where the UserAccountControl is "%%2089"

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4738 OR EventCode=4738 OR EventCode=2089)
| table _time, host, Account_Name, IpAddress, LogonType
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4738, 4738, 2089)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4738
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_per_000614 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "User Password Set to Never Expire"
    severity = "MEDIUM"
    tactic = "TA0003"
    technique = "T1098"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4738"

rule zs_pe_000260 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Change in GPO by Unauthorized User"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0004"
    technique = "T1484"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT @timestamp DESC
queries.leql:
where(logset = "Endpoint Activity")
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
having(event_count >= 1)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=ProcessRollup2
| stats count as proc_count by ComputerName, FileName
| where proc_count >= 1
| sort -proc_count
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" 
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

Where the events.name is "CREATE_ROLE"

author: TDE/TDE

name: Explorer Wermgr Potential Process Injection
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline. Tuning period: 14 days.
description: Detects suspicious command line arguments from potentially-injected versions of explorer or wermgr processes. This is a technique associated with QakBot malware. Investigate the parent process lineage to determine why these binaries are performing recon.
pseudo_logic:
1 Windows Process Creation Event
Where the Event ID is in [592, 4688]
Where Process Name in ["explorer.exe", "wermgr.exe"]
AND Command Line matches '.*(whoami\s*(-|\/)all|arp\s*(-|\/)a|ipconfig\s*(-|\/)all|net view\s*(-|\/)all|cmd \/c set|nslookup -querytype=all|nltest\s*(-|\/)domain_trusts\s*(-|\/)

queries.spl:
index=windows
| where (EventCode=4688 OR EventCode=4688)
| table _time, host, user, ProcessName, CommandLine
queries.kql:
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(1d)
| where EventID in (4688, 4688)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, IpAddress, EventID, Activity
queries.aql:
SELECT LOGSOURCENAME(logsourceid) AS log_source, username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid) AS event_name, eventcount
FROM events
WHERE eventid = 4688
  AND INOFFSET(starttime, LAST 300 SECONDS)
GROUP BY username, hostname, QIDNAME(qid)

ORDER BY starttime DESC
queries.yara_l:
rule zs_pe_000361 {
  meta:
    author = "TDE"
    description = "Explorer Wermgr Potential Process Injection"
    severity = "HIGH"
    tactic = "TA0004"
    technique = "T1055"

  events:
    $event.metadata.event_type = "GENERIC_EVENT"
    $event.metadata.product_event_type = "4688"
    $event.principal.hostname = $host
    $event.principal.user.userid = $user

  match:
    $host over 5m

  condition:
    #event >= 1

  outcome:
    $risk_score = max(50)
}
queries.esql:
FROM logs-windows.sysmon_operational*,logs-system.security*
| WHERE @timestamp > NOW() - 1 day
| WHERE event.code IN ('4688')
| STATS event_count = COUNT(*) BY host.name, user.name, event.code, @timestamp = BUCKET(@timestamp, 5m)
| WHERE event_count >= 1
| SORT event_count DESC
queries.leql:
where(event_id IN [4688])
groupby(hostname, username)
calculate(count AS event_count)
queries.crowdstrike:
event_simpleName=SecurityEvent
| where EventCode=4688
| table ComputerName, UserName, CommandLine, ContextTimeStamp
queries.xql:
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = "PROCESS" and action_evtlog_event_id in (4688)
| filter _time > to_timestamp(now() - 86400, "epoch")
| fields hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_name, action_process_command_line, _time
| sort desc _time

OR
	Command Line contains all of ["rundll32", ".dll,a", "/p"]

name: Kubernetes Pod Created with a hostPath Mount
tuning_guidance: Tune per environment baseline.
description:
Detects creation of a container with a hostPath mount. A hostPath volume mounts a directory or a file from the node to the container. Attackers who have permissions to create a new pod in the cluster may create one with a writable hostPath volume and chroot to escape to the underlying node. A sigma rule by Leo Tsaousis (@laripping)

ref: https://microsoft.github.io/Threat-Matrix-for-Kubernetes/tec
pseudo_logic:
1 Kubernetes Event 
Where the Resource is "pods"
Where Verb is "create"
Where "requestObject.spec.hostPath" is NOT Null
